summary: Backend-only testing for demo-user isolation on a dedicated demo restaurant (slug='demo') + translation cache. All 12 pytest cases pass. Verified: /api/seed is idempotent (re-running does not duplicate any data: restaurants/tables/menu_items/orders/menu_views/staff_calls counters unchanged), demo user logs in and sees ONLY Demo Restaurant (slug='demo', role=administrator), NO access to 'Мята Спортивная'/'Мята Центральная'. Public endpoints /api/public/demo-menu-info returns path='/demo/1', /api/public/demo-stats returns 1 restaurant, 8 tables, 25 menu items, >=45 orders, >=180 menu views, >=20 staff calls. Menu-by-slug /api/public/menu-by-slug/demo/1 serves 2 sections, 11 categories, 25 items. Demo restaurant shape verified via admin endpoints (2 menu_sections, 11 categories, 25 menu_items, 8 tables, 3 call_types). Translation cache: /api/translation-cache-stats returns {total>=63, seed=63}; requires auth (401/403 without token). Cache-hit latency verified directly against the service: cache_get ~0.3ms, translate_ru_to_en(use_cache=True) = 0.3ms (far under 100ms target). /api/translation-status smoke test passes (LLM reachable).
action_items: Consider updating /app/memory/test_credentials.md — it still says demo user is bound to Мята restaurants, which is now stale (demo is bound to slug='demo' only).
critical_code_review_comments: helpers.create_demo_user properly updates restaurant_ids if they drift (set comparison) — good idempotency guard.

demo_seed.ensure_demo_orders_and_calls has a subtle idempotency hole: the staff-calls branch uses `>= 15` as early-exit but the menu_views insert loop has no guard at all — every re-seed will add 180 more views. In practice it is gated by the outer `orders >= 30` check, so re-seed is safe today, but the menu-views loop should get its own guard for defense-in-depth (e.g., `if await db.menu_views.count_documents({'restaurant_id': rid}) >= 150: return`).

services/translation._ensure_indexes_and_seed uses a module-level `_seed_done` flag — safe for a single worker, but in multi-worker deployments the first insert_many can race; the unique index on key_ru is created first which correctly prevents duplicates (DuplicateKeyError) so re-seed is still effectively idempotent.
routes/seed.py: when demo user already exists with a stale restaurant_ids binding, create_demo_user updates it — verified working. Good.
test_credentials: admin/220066 (superadmin), demo/demo2026 (demo, administrator). /app/memory/test_credentials.md has stale info re: demo user restaurant binding (should be updated).
seed_data_creation: No new seed data created; /api/seed was re-invoked to verify idempotency — counters unchanged.
context_for_next_testing_agent: Test file at /app/backend/tests/test_demo_isolation_and_translation_cache.py is the regression suite for demo-isolation + translation cache. Demo restaurant id is resolved dynamically from demo user's restaurant_ids; tests do not hardcode ids. menu_views_total is asserted as >=180 because each /public/menu-by-slug hit increments it (normal app behavior). translation cache hit path was also validated via in-process script (cache_get ~0.3ms).
